# servicenow_js_include_relation (Resource)

`servicenow_js_include_relation` manages a relation between a js include and a widget dependency within ServiceNow.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Associates a JS include with a widget dependency for load ordering.
resource "servicenow_js_include" "example" {
  display_name = "example-js-include"
  source       = "url"
  url          = "https://example.com/script.js"
}

resource "servicenow_widget_dependency" "example" {
  name = "example-widget-dependency"
}

resource "servicenow_js_include_relation" "example" {
  js_include_id = servicenow_js_include.example.id
  dependency_id = servicenow_widget_dependency.example.id
  order         = 100
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `dependency_id` (String) Sys ID of the widget dependency this JS include is associated with.
- `js_include_id` (String) Sys ID of the JS include to associate with the widget dependency.

### Optional

- `order` (Number) The load order for the JS include within the widget dependency.
- `scope` (String) Associates a resource to a specific application ID in ServiceNow.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the sys_id of the record in ServiceNow.
terraform import servicenow_js_include_relation.example <sys_id>
```
